message:
feat: add centralized errors, CI, and documentation

- Add src/errors.zig with unified error types (MetalError, GGUFError, etc.)
- Add VERSION constant and --help/--version CLI flags to main.zig
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)
- Add CHANGELOG.md for version tracking
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md with development guidelines
- Add docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with system design overview

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,88 @@
+name: CI
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main]
+  pull_request:
+    branches: [main]
+
+jobs:
+  build:
+    name: Build and Test
+    runs-on: macos-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+
+      - name: Setup Zig
+        uses: goto-bus-stop/setup-zig@v2
+        with:
+          version: 0.15.0
+
+      - name: Check Formatting
+        run: |
+          zig fmt --check src/ || {
+            echo "::error::Code is not formatted. Run 'zig fmt src/' locally."
+            exit 1
+          }
+
+      - name: Build
+        run: zig build
+
+      - name: Run Unit Tests
+        run: zig build test
+        continue-on-error: false
+
+  lint:
+    name: Lint
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Check for common issues
+        run: |
+          # Check for TODO/FIXME comments that should be tracked
+          echo "Checking for untracked TODOs..."
+          grep -rn "TODO\|FIXME" src/ --include="*.zig" || true
+
+          # Check for debug prints that should be removed
+          echo "Checking for debug prints..."
+          grep -rn "std.debug.print" src/ --include="*.zig" | head -20 || true
+
+  # Integration tests require a model file, so we only run them on push to main
+  # and cache the test model
+  integration:
+    name: Integration Tests
+    runs-on: macos-latest
+    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
+
+    steps:
+      - name: Checkout
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          submodules: recursive
+
+      - name: Setup Zig
+        uses: goto-bus-stop/setup-zig@v2
+        with:
+          version: 0.15.0
+
+      - name: Build
+        run: zig build
+
+      - name: Build test executables
+        run: |
+          zig build test-paris 2>/dev/null || echo "test-paris requires model file"
+          zig build test-coherence 2>/dev/null || echo "test-coherence requires model file"
+
+      # Note: Full integration tests require large model files (26GB+)
+      # These are run locally or on self-hosted runners with model access
+      - name: Integration test placeholder
+        run: |
+          echo "Integration tests require model files."
+          echo "Run locally with: ./zig-out/bin/test-paris <model.gguf>"

@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# Changelog
+
+All notable changes to Zell will be documented in this file.
+
+The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
+and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
+
+## [Unreleased]
+
+### Added
+- Centralized error types in `src/errors.zig`
+- Global `--help` and `--version` CLI flags
+- VERSION constant in main.zig
+- GitHub Actions CI workflow
+- CONTRIBUTING.md guide
+- ARCHITECTURE.md documentation
+
+## [0.1.0] - 2024-12-26
+
+### Added
+- Initial release
+- MoE (Mixture of Experts) Metal GPU acceleration
+  - Hot expert pinning with Zipf-aware cache
+  - Oracle prefetch for SSD latency hiding
+  - Batched expert computation (8-10x speedup)
+- Layer-split speculative decoding for oversized models
+  - Run 34GB models on 16GB Mac
+  - Draft layers in GPU, target layers stream from SSD
+- System prompt caching (Radix Attention)
+  - 43.1% faster warm start (1.76x speedup)
+  - KV cache serialization to disk
+- Continuous batching scheduler
+  - Up to 32 parallel inference cells
+  - 3-4x throughput improvement
+- Automatic memory consolidation ("Sleep Cycle")
+  - Event-sourced conversation logging
+  - LoRA fine-tuning after 100 conversations
+  - Dual-adapter swap for zero downtime
+- OpenAI/Anthropic/Ollama-compatible HTTP API
+- Adaptive context sizing (512 to 131K tokens)
+- Tool execution (metal0/edgebox/lanceql)
+- LoRA adapter management
+- Snapshot/restore for instant boot
+
+### Infrastructure
+- Zig 0.15.2+ build system
+- llama.cpp integration (submodule)
+- Metal GPU compute kernels
+- 202 unit tests across 59 modules
+- 18 integration test programs
+- 4 benchmark suites

@@ -0,0 +1,166 @@
+# Contributing to Zell
+
+Thank you for your interest in contributing to Zell! This document provides guidelines and information for contributors.
+
+## Development Setup
+
+### Prerequisites
+
+- **macOS** (required for Metal GPU support)
+- **Zig 0.15.2+** - [Install Zig](https://ziglang.org/download/)
+- **Git** with submodule support
+
+### Building from Source
+
+```bash
+# Clone with submodules
+git clone --recursive https://github.com/teamchong/zell.git
+cd zell
+
+# Build
+zig build
+
+# Run tests
+zig build test
+
+# Run a specific test
+zig build test-paris
+```
+
+### Project Structure
+
+```
+zell/
+├── src/                    # Zig source code
+│   ├── main.zig           # CLI entry point
+│   ├── scheduler.zig      # Continuous batching scheduler
+│   ├── server.zig         # HTTP API server
+│   ├── moe_metal.zig      # MoE Metal GPU kernels
+│   ├── inference/         # Inference engines
+│   │   ├── gguf_loader.zig
+│   │   └── metal_inference.zig
+│   └── errors.zig         # Centralized error types
+├── vendor/llama.cpp/      # llama.cpp submodule
+├── build.zig              # Build configuration
+└── docs/                  # Documentation
+```
+
+## Code Style
+
+### Formatting
+
+Run `zig fmt` before committing:
+
+```bash
+zig fmt src/
+```
+
+### Naming Conventions
+
+- **Functions**: `camelCase` (e.g., `loadModel`, `runInference`)
+- **Types**: `PascalCase` (e.g., `MetalMoEEngine`, `GGUFLoader`)
+- **Constants**: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` or `snake_case` for compile-time values
+- **Files**: `snake_case.zig`
+
+### Error Handling
+
+Use error types from `src/errors.zig` when applicable:
+
+```zig
+const errors = @import("errors.zig");
+
+fn loadModel(path: []const u8) errors.InferenceError!Model {
+    // ...
+    return error.ModelNotFound;
+}
+```
+
+### Documentation
+
+- Add doc comments (`///`) for public functions and types
+- Include usage examples for complex APIs
+- Update CHANGELOG.md for user-visible changes
+
+## Testing
+
+### Running Tests
+
+```bash
+# All unit tests
+zig build test
+
+# Specific test binary
+zig build test-paris
+
+# Run with a model (required for integration tests)
+./zig-out/bin/test-paris ~/.zell/models/blobs/model.gguf
+```
+
+### Writing Tests
+
+Add inline tests for pure functions:
+
+```zig
+fn add(a: u32, b: u32) u32 {
+    return a + b;
+}
+
+test "add basic" {
+    try std.testing.expectEqual(@as(u32, 5), add(2, 3));
+}
+```
+
+For integration tests, create a new file in `src/` with the `test_` prefix.
+
+## Pull Request Process
+
+1. **Fork** the repository
+2. **Create a branch** for your feature (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`)
+3. **Make changes** and add tests
+4. **Run tests** (`zig build test`)
+5. **Format code** (`zig fmt src/`)
+6. **Commit** with a clear message
+7. **Push** and open a Pull Request
+
+### Commit Messages
+
+Follow conventional commit format:
+
+```
+feat(moe): add batched expert computation
+fix(loader): handle missing tensor gracefully
+perf(metal): optimize buffer allocation
+docs: update README with new examples
+test: add coverage for scheduler edge cases
+```
+
+## Areas for Contribution
+
+### Good First Issues
+
+- Add doc comments to undocumented functions
+- Improve error messages
+- Add unit tests for utilities
+- Fix typos in documentation
+
+### Medium Complexity
+
+- Add new quantization format support
+- Improve CLI help messages
+- Add benchmarks for specific operations
+
+### Advanced
+
+- Implement new GPU kernels
+- Optimize MoE routing
+- Add new model architecture support
+
+## Questions?
+
+- Open an issue for bugs or feature requests
+- Check existing issues before creating new ones
+- Join discussions in pull requests
+
+## License
+
+By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the same license as the project.

@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@
+# Zell Architecture
+
+This document provides a high-level overview of Zell's architecture for developers and contributors.
+
+## Overview
+
+Zell is a high-performance LLM inference engine that treats inference as a distributed system problem. It uses continuous batching, heterogeneous memory tiering, and speculative decoding to maximize hardware utilization.
+
+```
+┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│                         CLI / API                            │
+│  main.zig (CLI) ←→ server.zig (HTTP API)                    │
+└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+                              │
+                              ▼
+┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│                       Scheduler                              │
+│  scheduler.zig - Continuous batching with up to 32 cells    │
+└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+                              │
+          ┌───────────────────┼───────────────────┐
+          ▼                   ▼                   ▼
+┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐
+│   GPU (Metal)   │ │   CPU (SIMD)    │ │   SSD (mmap)    │
+│ moe_metal.zig   │ │ moe_simd.zig    │ │ gguf_loader.zig │
+│ metal_inference │ │ mixtral_lazy    │ │                 │
+└─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘ └─────────────────┘
+```
+
+## Core Components
+
+### 1. Scheduler (`scheduler.zig`)
+
+The scheduler implements continuous batching with a cell-based architecture:
+
+```zig
+pub const Cell = struct {
+    id: u32,           // Maps to llama seq_id
+    state: CellState,  // idle, thinking, waiting_for_tool, completed
+    buffer: []u8,      // Response buffer
+    pos: u32,          // Position in context
+};
+```
+
+**Key features:**
+- Up to 32 parallel inference cells
+- Paged attention (each cell gets a KV cache slice)
+- 3-4x throughput with proper batching
+
+### 2. Memory Tiering
+
+Zell uses three-tier memory management:
+
+| Tier | Storage | Contents | Access Time |
+|------|---------|----------|-------------|
+| Hot | GPU VRAM | 4 expert embeddings | ~1ms |
+| Warm | System RAM | Layer activations | ~10ms |
+| Cold | SSD mmap | Frozen weights | ~100ms |
+
+**Files:**
+- `budget_manager.zig` - Adaptive context sizing (512-131K tokens)
+- `memory_monitor.zig` - Runtime memory pressure detection
+- `expert_cache.zig` - Hot expert pinning for MoE
+
+### 3. MoE Engine (`moe_metal.zig`)
+
+The Mixture of Experts engine handles Mixtral-style models:
+
+```
+Token → Router → Top-K Experts → Combine → Output
+                    │
+                    ▼
+        ┌─────────────────────┐
+        │   Expert Cache      │
+        │ (4 hot in VRAM)     │
+        └─────────────────────┘
+```
+
+**Optimizations:**
+- Hot expert pinning (80% hit rate)
+- Oracle prefetch (predict during draft)
+- Dynamic Top-1 skip (avoid cold Top-2)
+- Batched computation (8-10x speedup)
+
+### 4. GGUF Loader (`inference/gguf_loader.zig`)
+
+Parses GGUF model files with support for:
+- Multiple quantization formats (Q2_K, Q4_K, Q6_K, Q8_0, FP16, BF16)
+- Memory-mapped weights (zero-copy)
+- Lazy loading for large models
+
+### 5. Layer-Split Speculative Decoding
+
+For running models larger than available RAM:
+
+```
+┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
+│   Draft Model    │     │   Target Model   │
+│   (GPU - fast)   │ ──▶ │   (SSD stream)   │
+│   20-25% layers  │     │   75-80% layers  │
+└──────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
+         │                        │
+         ▼                        ▼
+    Generate N           Verify all-at-once
+    draft tokens              (batch)
+```
+
+**Files:**
+- `layer_split.zig` - Split strategy
+- `speculative.zig` - Draft/verify loop
+
+### 6. Sleep Cycle (`hippocampus.zig`)
+
+Automatic memory consolidation and fine-tuning:
+
+```
+awake → drowsy → dreaming → deep_sleep → awake
+  │                  │           │
+  │                  │           └── Q-Healing (quant repair)
+  │                  └── LoRA training
+  └── Normal inference
+```
+
+**Files:**
+- `conversation_logger.zig` - Event-sourced JSONL logs
+- `hippocampus.zig` - State machine for sleep cycles
+- `train/lora_trainer.zig` - LoRA adapter training
+
+## Data Flow
+
+### Inference Request
+
+```
+1. HTTP Request (server.zig)
+       │
+2. Queue to Scheduler (scheduler.zig)
+       │
+3. Assign Cell (continuous batching)
+       │
+4. Load Weights (gguf_loader.zig)
+       │
+5. GPU Compute (moe_metal.zig / metal_inference.zig)
+       │
+6. Stream Response
+       │
+7. Log Conversation (conversation_logger.zig)
+```
+
+### Model Loading
+
+```
+1. Parse GGUF header
+       │
+2. Extract metadata (config, tokenizer)
+       │
+3. mmap weights (zero-copy)
+       │
+4. Create Metal buffers (hot experts)
+       │
+5. Load optimization profile
+```
+
+## Module Dependency Graph
+
+```
+main.zig
+├── server.zig
+│   └── scheduler.zig
+│       ├── llama.zig (C bindings)
+│       └── context_pool.zig
+├── optimizer.zig
+│   └── profile.zig
+├── moe_metal.zig
+│   ├── expert_cache.zig
+│   └── inference/metal_inference.zig
+├── hippocampus.zig
+│   ├── conversation_logger.zig
+│   └── train/lora_trainer.zig
+└── snapshot.zig
+```
+
+## Key Design Decisions
+
+### 1. Zig over C++
+
+- Comptime for zero-overhead abstractions
+- Explicit memory management (arena allocators)
+- C interop for llama.cpp
+
+### 2. Metal over CUDA
+
+- Native macOS GPU support
+- Unified memory architecture
+- Lower overhead for small batches
+
+### 3. Event Sourcing
+
+- Immutable conversation logs
+- Deterministic replay
+- Easy debugging and auditing
+
+### 4. Continuous Batching
+
+- Better GPU utilization
+- Lower latency for multiple users
+- Scales to 32+ concurrent requests
+
+## Performance Characteristics
+
+| Operation | Latency | Notes |
+|-----------|---------|-------|
+| Cold start | 11.3s | First inference |
+| Warm start | 6.4s | With cached system prompt |
+| Token generation | ~30 tok/s | MoE on 16GB Mac |
+| Expert cache hit | ~1ms | VRAM access |
+| Expert cache miss | ~100ms | SSD read |
+
+## Testing Strategy
+
+- **Unit tests**: Inline `test` blocks in modules
+- **Integration tests**: `test_*.zig` programs
+- **Benchmarks**: `bench_*.zig` programs
+- **CI**: GitHub Actions (macOS only)
+
+## Future Directions
+
+- Linux support (Vulkan compute backend)
+- Distributed inference across multiple GPUs
+- WebGPU for browser deployment
+- ONNX model support

@@ -0,0 +1,167 @@
+//! Centralized error types for Zell
+//!
+//! This module consolidates error types from across the codebase for consistency
+//! and discoverability. Individual modules can import and re-export these as needed.
+
+const std = @import("std");
+
+/// Errors related to Metal GPU operations
+pub const MetalError = error{
+    /// Failed to get Metal device handle
+    DeviceCreationFailed,
+    /// Failed to create Metal command queue
+    CommandQueueCreationFailed,
+    /// Metal shader compilation failed
+    ShaderCompilationFailed,
+    /// Failed to allocate Metal buffer
+    BufferAllocationFailed,
+    /// Failed to create command encoder
+    EncoderCreationFailed,
+    /// Failed to create command buffer
+    CommandBufferCreationFailed,
+    /// No stdout from child process
+    NoStdout,
+    /// Command execution failed
+    CommandFailed,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to GGUF file parsing and loading
+pub const GGUFError = error{
+    /// Invalid GGUF magic number
+    InvalidMagic,
+    /// Unsupported GGUF version
+    UnsupportedVersion,
+    /// Requested tensor not found in model
+    TensorNotFound,
+    /// Tensor has wrong type for operation
+    WrongTensorType,
+    /// Failed to parse GGUF file
+    ParseFailed,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to model inference
+pub const InferenceError = error{
+    /// Model file not found at path
+    ModelNotFound,
+    /// Out of memory during inference
+    OutOfMemory,
+    /// Invalid configuration for inference
+    InvalidConfiguration,
+    /// Failed to tokenize input
+    TokenizationFailed,
+    /// Failed to decode tokens
+    DecodeFailed,
+    /// Model path is invalid
+    InvalidModelPath,
+    /// Path is not a file
+    NotAFile,
+    /// Model not built (run zell build first)
+    ModelNotBuilt,
+    /// Profile failed to load
+    ProfileLoadFailed,
+    /// Vision not supported for this model
+    VisionNotSupported,
+    /// Missing mmproj for vision model
+    MissingMmproj,
+    /// General model load failure
+    ModelLoadFailed,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to file and I/O operations
+pub const IOError = error{
+    /// Failed to open file
+    FileOpenFailed,
+    /// Failed to read from file/stream
+    ReadFailed,
+    /// Failed to write to file/stream
+    WriteFailed,
+    /// Path is too long
+    PathTooLong,
+    /// Invalid file path
+    InvalidPath,
+    /// End of stream reached
+    EndOfStream,
+    /// Invalid offset in file
+    InvalidOffset,
+    /// HTTP request failed
+    HttpError,
+    /// Network operation failed
+    NetworkError,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to CLI arguments
+pub const ArgumentError = error{
+    /// Invalid command line argument
+    InvalidArgument,
+    /// Required argument is missing
+    MissingArgument,
+    /// Invalid arguments provided
+    InvalidArguments,
+    /// Unknown subcommand
+    UnknownSubcommand,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to snapshot/cache operations
+pub const SnapshotError = error{
+    /// Invalid snapshot file format
+    InvalidSnapshotFile,
+    /// Invalid snapshot magic number
+    InvalidSnapshotMagic,
+    /// Unsupported snapshot version
+    UnsupportedSnapshotVersion,
+    /// Snapshot doesn't match current model
+    ModelMismatch,
+    /// Cache is stale and needs regeneration
+    CacheStale,
+    /// Invalid freeze file format
+    InvalidFreezeFile,
+    /// Invalid freeze magic number
+    InvalidFreezeMagic,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to LoRA adapters
+pub const LoRAError = error{
+    /// Adapter not found
+    AdapterNotFound,
+    /// Failed to load adapter
+    AdapterLoadFailed,
+    /// Invalid adapter format
+    InvalidAdapter,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to server/API operations
+pub const ServerError = error{
+    /// Invalid JSON in request
+    InvalidJson,
+    /// Missing max_tokens in request
+    MissingMaxTokens,
+    /// Invalid max_tokens value
+    InvalidMaxTokens,
+    /// Missing messages in request
+    MissingMessages,
+    /// Invalid messages format
+    InvalidMessages,
+};
+
+/// Errors related to environment/system
+pub const SystemError = error{
+    /// HOME environment variable not set
+    NoHomeDir,
+    /// No quantization pattern found in model name
+    NoQuantizationPattern,
+    /// No reference model found for Q-Healing
+    NoReferenceModelFound,
+};
+
+/// Combined error set for common operations
+pub const ZellError = MetalError || GGUFError || InferenceError || IOError ||
+    ArgumentError || SnapshotError || LoRAError || ServerError || SystemError;
+
+test "error types are distinct" {
+    // Ensure error types can be used in error unions
+    const TestFn = fn () MetalError!void;
+    _ = TestFn;
+
+    const TestFn2 = fn () ZellError!void;
+    _ = TestFn2;
+}
